repo.name: psf-requests
setup: No dependency setup is required for read-only benchmark inspection.

tasks.prompt: Explain how Requests turns a top-level request call into a prepared request and sends it through a session adapter. Cite the source files and name the supporting symbols.

expected_symbols.name: request
expected_symbols.why: Top-level helper that creates a Session and delegates request execution.

expected_symbols.name: Session
expected_symbols.why: Owns request preparation, adapter lookup, and sending.

expected_symbols.name: Session.request
expected_symbols.why: Builds and prepares the Request before sending.

expected_symbols.name: Session.send
expected_symbols.why: Dispatches a PreparedRequest through the selected adapter.

expected_symbols.name: PreparedRequest.prepare
expected_symbols.why: Materializes method, URL, headers, body, auth, cookies, and hooks.

expected_claims.text: The top-level request helper opens a Session and delegates to Session.request.
Session.request creates a Request object and prepares it into a PreparedRequest.
Session.send chooses an adapter and calls the adapter send method.
HTTPAdapter.send is the transport boundary that returns the response.

forbidden_claims.text: The top-level request helper sends directly over the network without a Session.

repo.name: apache-commons-lang
setup: No dependency setup is required for read-only benchmark inspection.

tasks.prompt: Explain how Commons Lang implements blank, empty, and case-sensitive string checks across StringUtils, Strings, and CharSequenceUtils. Cite the source files and name the supporting symbols.

expected_claims.text: StringUtils.isBlank treats null, empty, and whitespace-only inputs as blank.
StringUtils.isEmpty does not trim whitespace before deciding emptiness.
Strings delegates region matching work to CharSequenceUtils.regionMatches.

forbidden_claims.text: StringUtils.isEmpty treats whitespace-only strings as empty.

repo.name: BurntSushi-ripgrep
setup: No dependency setup is required for read-only benchmark inspection.

tasks.prompt: Explain how ripgrep parses CLI flags, walks candidate files, and executes a search over each haystack through matcher, searcher, and printer components. Cite the source files and name the supporting symbols.

expected_symbols.name: run
expected_symbols.why: Routes parsed args to the selected operating mode.

expected_symbols.name: search
expected_symbols.why: Single-threaded walk and per-file search loop.

expected_claims.text: main calls run after flags are parsed.
HiArgs builds walkers, matchers, searchers, and printers for the search driver.
SearchWorker connects a matcher, grep searcher, and printer for each haystack.
Parallel search uses the walker parallel builder to distribute file work.

forbidden_claims.text: Flag parsing happens inside SearchWorker instead of before run selects a mode.

repo.name: expressjs-express
setup: No dependency setup is required for read-only benchmark inspection.

tasks.prompt: Trace how Express creates an application, registers middleware/routes, and handles an incoming request through the router and response helpers. Cite the source files and name the supporting symbols.

expected_claims.text: createApplication builds a callable app object and mixes in request and response prototypes.
app.use registers middleware on the router.
app.handle delegates request handling to the router.
res.send prepares and sends the response body.

forbidden_claims.text: Express handles requests without delegating to a router.

repo.name: vercel-swr
setup: No dependency setup is required for read-only benchmark inspection.

tasks.prompt: Explain how SWR exposes useSWR, serializes keys, connects cache helpers, and routes mutate behavior through the internal mutation helper. Cite the source files and name the supporting symbols.

expected_symbols.name: createCacheHelper
expected_symbols.why: Builds cache accessors for SWR state.

expected_claims.text: The public useSWR export wraps useSWRHandler with argument normalization.
useSWRHandler serializes the key before reading cache state.
createCacheHelper provides cache get, set, subscribe, and snapshot helpers.
mutate behavior flows through internalMutate.

forbidden_claims.text: SWR stores hook state without serializing the cache key.

repo.name: fmtlib-fmt
setup: No dependency setup is required for read-only benchmark inspection.

tasks.prompt: Explain how fmt turns formatting arguments into type-erased format args and reaches vformat or format_to output paths. Cite the source files and name the supporting symbols.

expected_symbols.name: format_to
expected_symbols.why: Writes formatted output to an iterator.

expected_claims.text: fmt builds type-erased format argument stores before dispatching formatting.
vformat is the central formatting path for runtime format arguments.
format_to writes formatted output through an output iterator.
format_error represents formatting failures.

forbidden_claims.text: fmt only supports compile-time formatting and has no runtime vformat path.

repo.name: redis-redis
setup: No dependency setup is required for read-only benchmark inspection.

tasks.prompt: Trace how Redis initializes the server, enters the event loop, reads client input, and routes a command for execution. Cite the source files and name the supporting symbols.

expected_symbols.name: initServer
expected_symbols.why: Initializes server state and event handlers.

expected_claims.text: Redis main initializes server state before entering the event loop.
aeMain drives registered file and time events.
readQueryFromClient reads client input into query buffers.
processCommand performs command routing and execution checks.

forbidden_claims.text: Redis executes commands directly from the TCP accept path without the event loop.

repo.name: gin-gonic-gin
setup: No dependency setup is required for read-only benchmark inspection.

tasks.prompt: Trace how Gin creates an engine, registers routes through router groups, stores them in method trees, and dispatches handlers for a request. Cite the source files and name the supporting symbols.

expected_symbols.name: Default
expected_symbols.why: Creates an Engine with default middleware.

expected_symbols.name: Engine.addRoute
expected_symbols.why: Adds route handlers to method trees.

expected_symbols.name: node.addRoute
expected_symbols.why: Inserts a route into the radix tree.

expected_symbols.name: Engine.handleHTTPRequest
expected_symbols.why: Matches request paths and dispatches handlers.

expected_claims.text: RouterGroup.Handle registers routes by delegating to the group handle path.
Engine.addRoute inserts handlers into the per-method route tree.
Engine.handleHTTPRequest finds a route and installs handlers on the context.
Context.Next advances through the handler chain.

forbidden_claims.text: Gin dispatches handlers without storing routes in method trees.

repo.name: jekyll-jekyll
setup: No dependency setup is required for read-only benchmark inspection.

tasks.prompt: Trace how Jekyll's build command creates a site and runs the read, generate, render, and write phases. Cite the source files and name the supporting symbols.

expected_claims.text: Build.process constructs or processes a Jekyll site.
Site.process runs reset, read, generate, render, cleanup, and write phases.
Reader is responsible for reading site content.
Renderer renders pages and documents.

forbidden_claims.text: Jekyll writes output before reading and rendering the site.

repo.name: Seldaek-monolog
setup: No dependency setup is required for read-only benchmark inspection.

tasks.prompt: Explain how Monolog turns a log call into a LogRecord and passes it through handlers. Cite the source files and name the supporting symbols.

expected_symbols.name: Logger
expected_symbols.why: Public logging API and handler owner.

expected_claims.text: Logger owns a stack of handlers registered by pushHandler.
Logger::log delegates into addRecord.
addRecord creates a LogRecord before passing it to handlers.
AbstractProcessingHandler handles records by processing and writing them.

forbidden_claims.text: Monolog sends log messages directly to handlers without creating a LogRecord.

repo.name: AutoMapper-AutoMapper
setup: No dependency setup is required for read-only benchmark inspection.

tasks.prompt: Explain how AutoMapper configuration and runtime mapper APIs cooperate to map source objects to destination objects. Cite the source files and name the supporting symbols.

expected_claims.text: MapperConfiguration builds and owns the mapping configuration used at runtime.
Mapper.Map is the public runtime entry point for object mapping.
TypeMap contributes mapper lambda plans used by the execution pipeline.
TypeMapPlanBuilder participates in building expression plans for mappings.

forbidden_claims.text: AutoMapper maps objects without using configuration or type maps.

repo.name: square-okio
setup: No dependency setup is required for read-only benchmark inspection.

tasks.prompt: Explain how Okio's Buffer, Source, Sink, and buffered wrappers cooperate to move bytes through reads and writes. Cite the source files and name the supporting symbols.

expected_symbols.name: buffer
expected_symbols.why: Wraps raw sources or sinks in buffered implementations.

expected_claims.text: Buffer is the in-memory byte store used by Okio reads and writes.
RealBufferedSource reads from an upstream Source into a Buffer.
RealBufferedSink writes buffered bytes to an upstream Sink.
Okio buffer helpers wrap Source and Sink instances with buffered implementations.

forbidden_claims.text: Okio buffered wrappers bypass Buffer and write every byte directly.

repo.name: Alamofire-Alamofire
setup: No dependency setup is required for read-only benchmark inspection.

tasks.prompt: Trace how Alamofire's Session creates requests, resumes tasks, validates data requests, and receives URLSession callbacks. Cite the source files and name the supporting symbols.

expected_symbols.name: Session
expected_symbols.why: Owns request creation and URLSession integration.

expected_claims.text: Session creates request objects such as DataRequest.
Request.resume resumes the underlying URLSession task.
DataRequest.validate attaches validation behavior.
SessionDelegate receives URLSession callback events.

forbidden_claims.text: Alamofire sends requests without a Session or URLSession delegate path.

repo.name: dart-lang-http
setup: No dependency setup is required for read-only benchmark inspection.

tasks.prompt: Explain how package:http exposes top-level helpers, BaseClient convenience methods, BaseRequest finalization, and IOClient send behavior. Cite the source files and name the supporting symbols.

expected_claims.text: Top-level package:http helpers delegate to a Client.
BaseClient implements convenience methods in terms of send.
BaseRequest.finalize prepares the request body for sending.
IOClient.send is the dart:io transport implementation.

forbidden_claims.text: package:http top-level helpers bypass Client and IOClient entirely.

repo.name: nvm-sh-nvm
setup: No dependency setup is required for read-only benchmark inspection.

tasks.prompt: Trace how nvm's install script bootstraps the shell function and how nvm.sh dispatches install, download, and use commands. Cite the source files and name the supporting symbols.

expected_claims.text: install.sh bootstraps installation and arranges for nvm.sh to be sourced.
nvm.sh defines the main nvm dispatcher function.
nvm_install_node and nvm_download participate in installing Node versions.
nvm_use_if_needed switches versions only when the requested version is not already active.

forbidden_claims.text: nvm is a compiled binary and does not dispatch through shell functions.

repo.name: mdn-learning-area
setup: No dependency setup is required for read-only benchmark inspection.

tasks.prompt: Explain how the MDN form validation examples combine native HTML constraints with custom JavaScript validation. Cite the source files and name the supporting elements or functions.

expected_symbols.name: novalidate
expected_symbols.why: Disables browser UI for custom validation.

expected_claims.text: The examples use native required, pattern, min, and max constraints.
The detailed custom validation example uses novalidate to suppress the browser default UI.
The showError function branches on ValidityState fields to choose messages.
Submit handlers prevent submission when the form is invalid.

forbidden_claims.text: The examples rely only on JavaScript and do not use native HTML constraints.

repo.name: animate-css-animate-css
setup: No dependency setup is required for read-only benchmark inspection.

tasks.prompt: Explain how animate.css defines shared animation variables/base classes and connects named animation classes to keyframes. Cite the source files and name the supporting selectors or keyframes.

expected_claims.text: Shared CSS custom properties define animation duration, delay, and repeat defaults.
.animate__animated is the base class that applies animation duration and fill mode.
Named classes such as .animate__bounce set animation-name to matching keyframes.
The source/animate.css file imports the variable, base, and individual animation files.

forbidden_claims.text: animate.css defines animations only in JavaScript and not in CSS keyframes.

repo.name: lerocha-chinook-database
setup: No dependency setup is required for read-only benchmark inspection.

tasks.prompt: Explain the core Chinook schema relationships between artists, albums, tracks, invoices, and invoice lines across the SQL seed scripts. Cite the source files and name the supporting tables or constraints.

expected_claims.text: Album rows reference Artist rows through ArtistId.
Track rows reference Album, MediaType, and Genre rows.
InvoiceLine rows reference Invoice and Track rows.
The repository carries multiple SQL dialect scripts for the same Chinook schema.

forbidden_claims.text: The Chinook SQL scripts define tables without foreign key relationships.
